other_observers: John Vanderpoel and one other person whose name I did not know.; John Vanderpoel is the original observer of the bird. To my knowledge Norm Erthel and Joe Roller also observed the bird at the same location on 08/19/2007 around 3:30 PM.
description: Hummingbird, approximately the size of a Broad-tailed Hummingbird. Gorget color: red. Deeply forked tail when perched and deeply forked tail when spread in a hover. No wing buzz when flying. Tail irregularly bobbed during feeding.Behaviors: The bird was perched on a fence near a hummingbird feeder. The bird was also seen feeding at a hummingbird feeder. When feeding whilst hovering, the bird bobbed its posterior in an irregular manner and less emphatically than a Black-chinned Hummingbird.Call: None heardPlumage: Breeding
